data.description:
TTW Announces Top 50 Amusement Park Travel Destinations in the World for 2026

TTW Announces Top 50 Amusement Park Travel Destinations in the World for 2026

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, June 13, 2026

NEW YORK, June 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Travel And Tour World (TTW) today announced its Top 50 Amusement Park Travel Destinations in the World for 2026. Crafted by TTW's editorial experts, the report reflects the preferences of a global audience increasingly drawn to world-class theme parks, immersive entertainment, record-breaking attractions, family adventures, and unforgettable experiences that transform ordinary holidays into extraordinary journeys.

TTW's 2026 ranking names the United States as the world's leading amusement park travel destination, followed by Canada, Mexico, France, and Japan, while Germany, the United Kingdom, Spain, China, and Singapore complete the top ten. Spanning North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Latin America, the Middle East, and Africa, the Top 50 showcases destinations excelling in theme parks, water attractions, immersive entertainment, family experiences, and tourism innovation, reflecting the growing global demand for world-class amusement park travel experiences.

The global appetite for entertainment-driven travel is reshaping tourism preferences, with more travelers choosing destinations that offer world-class theme parks, immersive attractions, family adventures, and unforgettable experiences. Visitors are increasingly seeking destinations known for iconic amusement parks, record-breaking roller coasters, water attractions, movie-themed worlds, and cutting-edge entertainment districts.

"Amusement parks have become some of the world's most influential tourism attractions, combining entertainment, innovation, and immersive storytelling to appeal to travelers of all ages. As global uncertainties and ongoing conflicts shape travel decisions, multigenerational visitors increasingly seek safe destinations that offer escapism, excitement, and meaningful shared experiences.

From thrilling rides to themed environments rooted in culture, history, and imagination, amusement parks create lasting memories while encouraging family and multi-generational travel.

Beyond entertainment, they serve as powerful tourism drivers, attracting visitors, extending stays, increasing spending, and supporting broader economic growth across hospitality, transportation, retail, and local communities," says Anup Kumar Keshan, Founder and Editor-in-Chief of Travel and Tour World.

Detailed Overview

1. USA

USA remains the world's leading amusement park destination in 2026, offering iconic Disney parks, immersive movie-themed attractions, record-breaking roller coasters, marine-life experiences, wildlife adventures, and family resorts across Orlando, Anaheim, and Los Angeles. It leads global amusement park tourism with unmatched theme park variety, innovation, iconic attractions, and year-round entertainment. It leads the global amusement park industry with more than 400 amusement and theme parks spread across the country. 

Best Time to Visit
Spring and autumn provide pleasant weather and lighter crowds, while summer features extended operating hours, seasonal events, and special entertainment.

Top 10 Amusement Parks

1. Magic Kingdom, Orlando, Florida
Home to Cinderella Castle, TRON Lightcycle / Run, Space Mountain, themed lands, parades, and fireworks.
How to Reach There: Fly into Orlando International Airport and continue by shuttle, taxi or rideshare.

2. Disneyland Park, Anaheim, California
Features Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge, Indiana Jones Adventure, Space Mountain, beloved characters, and classic Disney storytelling.
How to Reach There: Fly into John Wayne or Los Angeles International Airport.

3. EPCOT, Orlando, Florida
Known for World Showcase, international culture, festivals, dining, Spaceship Earth, and Guardians of the Galaxy: Cosmic Rewind.
How to Reach There: Fly into Orlando International Airport.

4. Disney's Hollywood Studios, Orlando, Florida
Highlights Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge, Rise of the Resistance, Toy Story Land, and Tower of Terror.
How to Reach There: Fly into Orlando International Airport.

5. Universal's Islands of Adventure, Orlando, Florida
Famous for VelociCoaster, Hagrid's Motorbike Adventure, Marvel attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Orlando International Airport.

6. Disney California Adventure, Anaheim, California
Features Avengers Campus, Radiator Springs Racers and Guardians of the Galaxy – Mission: Breakout!
How to Reach There: Fly into John Wayne, Long Beach, or Los Angeles International Airport.

7. Universal Studios Florida, Orlando, Florida
Offers Harry Potter and the Escape from Gringotts, Transformers, live shows, and movie-inspired rides.
How to Reach There: Fly into Orlando International Airport.

8. Universal Studios Hollywood, Los Angeles, California
Combines a working film studio with Studio Tour, Super Nintendo World, Jurassic World, and Harry Potter attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Los Angeles International Airport or Hollywood Burbank Airport.

9. Disney's Animal Kingdom, Orlando, Florida
Blends wildlife, conservation, Pandora – The World of Avatar, Kilimanjaro Safaris, and Expedition Everest.
How to Reach There: Fly into Orlando International Airport.

10. SeaWorld Orlando, Orlando, Florida
Known for marine-life encounters and major coasters including Mako, Pipeline, and Ice Breaker.
How to Reach There: Fly into Orlando International Airport and continue to the International Drive area.

2. Canada

Canada is one of the world's top amusement park destinations in 2026, offering thrilling roller coasters, family attractions, waterfront entertainment, wildlife experiences, and seasonal festivals across Ontario, Quebec, Alberta, and British Columbia. Canada combines world-class thrill rides, family-friendly attractions, scenic settings, and safe, well-developed tourism infrastructure. Canada is home to an estimated 100 amusement and theme parks, ranging from major attractions to regional family destinations.

Best Time to Visit
Late spring through early autumn offers pleasant weather, while summer delivers the full range of rides, events, and outdoor entertainment.

Top 10 Amusement Parks

1. Canada's Wonderland, Vaughan, Ontario
Home to Leviathan, Yukon Striker, Behemoth, Splash Works, and over 200 attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Toronto Pearson International Airport and continue by car, bus, or taxi.

2. La Ronde, Montreal, Quebec
Features Goliath, Vampire, family rides, seasonal events, and riverside views.
How to Reach There: Fly into Montréal–Trudeau International Airport and travel to Île Sainte-Hélène.

3. Calaway Park, Calgary, Alberta
Known for family rides, roller coasters, live entertainment, and children's attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Calgary International Airport and continue by car or shuttle.

4. Galaxyland, Edmonton, Alberta
An indoor amusement park featuring Mindbender, family rides, and entertainment zones.
How to Reach There: Fly into Edmonton International Airport and travel to West Edmonton Mall.

5. Playland, Vancouver, British Columbia
Offers classic wooden coasters, thrill rides, midway games, and seasonal events.
How to Reach There: Fly into Vancouver International Airport and continue to Hastings Park.

6. Centreville Amusement Park, Toronto Islands, Ontario
Popular for family rides, scenic island surroundings, and children's attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Toronto Pearson International Airport and take a ferry to Toronto Islands.

7. Village Vacances Valcartier, Quebec City, Quebec
Combines water park attractions, family rides, and seasonal outdoor adventures.
How to Reach There: Fly into Québec City Jean Lesage International Airport.

8. Cultus Lake Adventure Park, British Columbia
Features roller coasters, family rides, interactive attractions, and themed experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Vancouver International Airport and continue by road.

9. Santa's Village, Bracebridge, Ontario
A family-focused park offering themed rides, water attractions, and holiday experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Toronto Pearson International Airport and travel north by car.

3. Mexico

Mexico is one of Latin America's leading amusement park destinations in 2026, offering world-class thrill rides, family attractions, water parks, cultural themes, and entertainment complexes across Mexico City, Guadalajara, Monterrey, and Cancun. Mexico combines thrilling attractions, cultural experiences, affordable entertainment, and year-round warm weather. It features approximately 90 amusement and theme parks that support its growing entertainment tourism sector.

Best Time to Visit
November to April offers pleasant weather and comfortable sightseeing conditions, while summer brings water park experiences and family holiday travel.

Top 10 Amusement Parks

1. Six Flags México, Mexico City
Home to Superman El Último Escape, Medusa Steel Coaster, Batman The Ride, and major thrill attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Mexico City International Airport and continue by taxi or rideshare.

2. Xcaret Park, Riviera Maya, Quintana Roo
Features underground rivers, wildlife, cultural performances, beaches, and eco-adventures.
How to Reach There: Fly into Cancun International Airport and travel south to Riviera Maya.

3. Xplor Park, Riviera Maya, Quintana Roo
Known for zip lines, underground caves, amphibious vehicles, and adventure activities.
How to Reach There: Fly into Cancun International Airport and continue by road.

4. Selva Mágica, Guadalajara, Jalisco
Offers roller coasters, family rides, children's attractions, and entertainment zones.
How to Reach There: Fly into Guadalajara International Airport.

5. Parque Fundidora and Plaza Sésamo, Monterrey, Nuevo León
Combines family rides, themed attractions, water experiences, and recreational spaces.
How to Reach There: Fly into Monterrey International Airport and continue to Fundidora Park.

6. Ventura Park, Cancun, Quintana Roo
Features water slides, zip lines, go-karts, virtual reality attractions, and family entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Cancun International Airport.

7. Kataplum, Mexico City
Known for roller coasters, family rides, panoramic attractions, and urban entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Mexico City International Airport.

8. Parque Acuático El Rollo, Morelos
Popular for wave pools, water slides, family attractions, and aquatic adventures.
How to Reach There: Fly into Mexico City International Airport and continue by road.

9. Reino Animal, State of Mexico
Combines safari experiences, wildlife encounters, educational attractions, and family activities.
How to Reach There: Fly into Mexico City International Airport and travel by car.

10. Granja Las Américas, Mexico City
A family-focused attraction featuring educational activities, themed zones, and interactive experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Mexico City International Airport and continue by taxi or rideshare.

4. France

France is Europe's premier amusement park destination in 2026, offering world-famous Disney experiences, historical themes, immersive storytelling, family attractions, and cutting-edge thrill rides across Paris and other major regions. France combines world-class themed entertainment, rich cultural storytelling, iconic attractions, and exceptional visitor experiences. It boasts more than 120 amusement and theme parks, including some of Europe's most visited attractions.

Best Time to Visit
Spring and autumn offer pleasant weather and shorter queues, while summer brings extended hours, festivals, and seasonal entertainment.

Top 10 Amusement Parks

1. Disneyland Park, Marne-la-Vallée, Paris
Home to Sleeping Beauty Castle, Pirates of the Caribbean, Big Thunder Mountain, and Star Wars attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport and take direct rail or shuttle services.

2. Walt Disney Studios Park, Marne-la-Vallée, Paris
Features Avengers Campus, Ratatouille Adventure, Tower of Terror, and movie-themed attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport and continue by train or shuttle.

3. Parc Astérix, Plailly, Hauts-de-France
Known for Toutatis, OzIris, themed lands, family rides, and Gaul-inspired adventures.
How to Reach There: Fly into Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport and travel by shuttle or car.

4. Puy du Fou, Les Epesses, Vendée
Famous for spectacular historical shows, live performances, medieval villages, and immersive experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Nantes Atlantique Airport and continue by road.

5. Futuroscope, Poitiers, Nouvelle-Aquitaine
Features multimedia attractions, immersive technology, flying theatres, and futuristic experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Poitiers Airport or travel by high-speed rail.

6. Nigloland, Dolancourt, Grand Est
Offers roller coasters, family rides, water attractions, and themed zones.
How to Reach There: Fly into Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport and continue by car.

7. Le PAL, Saint-Pourçain-sur-Besbre, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes
Combines amusement rides, wildlife encounters, roller coasters, and family attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Lyon-Saint Exupéry Airport and continue by road.

8. Walibi Rhône-Alpes, Les Avenières, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes
Known for thrill rides, Mystic coaster, water attractions, and family entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Lyon-Saint Exupéry Airport.

9. Parc Spirou Provence, Monteux, Provence-Alpes-Côte d'Azur
Features comic-book themed rides, family attractions, simulators, and entertainment zones.
How to Reach There: Fly into Marseille Provence Airport and continue by road.

10. Jardin d'Acclimatation, Paris
Offers family rides, gardens, attractions, playgrounds, and cultural experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport and travel by metro or taxi.

5. Japan

Japan is one of Asia's leading amusement park destinations in 2026, offering world-famous theme parks, immersive entertainment, cutting-edge technology, anime-inspired attractions, and family-friendly experiences across Tokyo, Osaka, Nagoya, and Yokohama. Japan combines innovative attractions, world-class themed entertainment, beloved characters, and exceptional visitor experiences. Japan's entertainment landscape includes an estimated 200 amusement and theme parks, reflecting its strong theme park culture.

Best Time to Visit
Spring and autumn provide pleasant weather and comfortable sightseeing, while summer features seasonal events, festivals, and extended park hours.

Top 10 Amusement Parks

1. Tokyo Disneyland, Urayasu, Chiba
Home to Cinderella Castle, Pooh's Hunny Hunt, Splash Mountain, and classic Disney attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Tokyo Narita or Haneda Airport and take rail connections to Tokyo Disney Resort.

2. Tokyo DisneySea, Urayasu, Chiba
Known for unique themed ports, Journey to the Center of the Earth, Soaring, and world-class storytelling.
How to Reach There: Fly into Tokyo Narita or Haneda Airport.

3. Universal Studios Japan, Osaka
Features Super Nintendo World, The Wizarding World of Harry Potter, Minions, and blockbuster attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Kansai International Airport and continue by train.

4. Fuji-Q Highland, Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi
Famous for record-breaking roller coasters, thrilling rides, and Mount Fuji views.
How to Reach There: Fly into Tokyo Haneda Airport and travel by bus or train.

5. Nagashima Spa Land, Kuwana, Mie
Offers world-class roller coasters, water parks, family rides, and resort facilities.
How to Reach There: Fly into Chubu Centrair International Airport near Nagoya.

6. Yokohama Cosmo World, Yokohama
Known for Cosmo Clock 21 Ferris wheel, family rides, and waterfront entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Tokyo Haneda Airport and continue by rail.

7. Legoland Japan Resort, Nagoya
Features LEGO-themed attractions, interactive exhibits, family rides, and entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Chubu Centrair International Airport.

8. Huis Ten Bosch, Sasebo, Nagasaki
A Dutch-themed park offering attractions, gardens, illuminations, and entertainment experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Nagasaki Airport and continue by road or rail.

9. Yomiuriland, Tokyo
Offers roller coasters, seasonal events, family attractions, and nighttime illuminations.
How to Reach There: Fly into Tokyo Haneda or Narita Airport and continue by rail.

10. Sanrio Puroland, Tokyo
Home to Hello Kitty, character-themed attractions, live shows, and family entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Tokyo Haneda or Narita Airport and travel by train to Tama City.

6. Germany

Germany is one of Europe's top amusement park destinations in 2026, offering world-class roller coasters, immersive themed lands, family attractions, water rides, and entertainment experiences across Rust, Brühl, Günzburg, and other major regions. Germany combines record-breaking attractions, innovative themed experiences, family entertainment, and exceptional value.Germany operates more than 250 amusement and theme parks, making it one of Europe's largest leisure markets.

Best Time to Visit
Late spring through early autumn offers pleasant weather and full park operations, while summer features festivals and extended opening hours.

Top 10 Amusement Parks

1. Europa-Park, Rust, Baden-Württemberg
Home to Voltron Nevera, Silver Star, Blue Fire, themed European lands, and world-class entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Strasbourg Airport or Frankfurt Airport and continue by road or shuttle.

2. Phantasialand, Brühl, North Rhine-Westphalia
Features Taron, F.L.Y., Black Mamba, immersive themed worlds, and family attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Cologne Bonn Airport and continue by train or taxi.

3. Heide Park Resort, Soltau, Lower Saxony
Known for Colossos, Flug der Dämonen, family rides, and adventure-themed attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Hamburg Airport and continue by road or rail.

4. LEGOLAND Deutschland Resort, Günzburg, Bavaria
Offers LEGO-themed attractions, family rides, interactive exhibits, and entertainment zones.
How to Reach There: Fly into Munich Airport and continue by train or car.

5. Hansa-Park, Sierksdorf, Schleswig-Holstein
Features Kärnan, Schwur des Kärnan, family rides, and Baltic Sea views.
How to Reach There: Fly into Hamburg Airport and continue by rail or road.

6. Movie Park Germany, Bottrop, North Rhine-Westphalia
Known for movie-themed attractions, roller coasters, stunt shows, and family entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Düsseldorf Airport and continue by train or car.

7. Tripsdrill Adventure Park, Cleebronn, Baden-Württemberg
Offers themed roller coasters, family rides, wildlife experiences, and German heritage attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Stuttgart Airport and continue by road.

8. Bayern-Park, Reisbach, Bavaria
Features thrill rides, family attractions, water rides, and outdoor entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Munich Airport and continue by car.

9. Serengeti Park, Hodenhagen, Lower Saxony
Combines safari experiences, amusement rides, wildlife encounters, and family attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Hanover Airport and continue by road.

10. Erlebnispark Schloss Thurn, Heroldsbach, Bavaria
Known for family rides, themed attractions, water experiences, and live entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Nuremberg Airport and continue by car or shuttle.

7. United Kingdom

The United Kingdom is one of Europe's leading amusement park destinations in 2026, offering world-famous roller coasters, immersive themed lands, family attractions, adventure parks, and entertainment resorts across England, Scotland, and Wales. The UK combines iconic thrill rides, family entertainment, rich themes, and some of Europe's most celebrated theme parks. The United Kingdom is estimated to have over 180 amusement and theme parks serving domestic and international visitors.

Best Time to Visit
Late spring through early autumn offers the best weather and full park operations, while summer brings seasonal events and extended opening hours.

Top 10 Amusement Parks

1. Alton Towers Resort, Staffordshire, England
Home to Nemesis Reborn, Wicker Man, Oblivion, The Smiler, and immersive themed areas.
How to Reach There: Fly into Manchester Airport or Birmingham Airport and continue by road.

2. Thorpe Park Resort, Surrey, England
Features Hyperia, Stealth, Saw – The Ride, Nemesis Inferno, and major thrill attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into London Heathrow Airport and continue by road or rail.

3. Chessington World of Adventures, Surrey, England
Combines roller coasters, family rides, themed lands, and a wildlife zoo.
How to Reach There: Fly into London Heathrow Airport and continue by train or taxi.

4. Blackpool Pleasure Beach, Lancashire, England
Known for ICON, Big One, Revolution, Valhalla, and classic seaside entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Manchester Airport and continue by rail or road.

5. LEGOLAND Windsor Resort, Berkshire, England
Offers LEGO-themed attractions, interactive experiences, family rides, and entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into London Heathrow Airport and continue by road.

6. Paultons Park, Hampshire, England
Home to Peppa Pig World, family attractions, roller coasters, and themed experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Southampton Airport and continue by car.

7. Drayton Manor Resort, Staffordshire, England
Features family rides, thrill attractions, Thomas Land, and zoo experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Birmingham Airport and continue by road.

8. Flamingo Land Resort, North Yorkshire, England
Combines roller coasters, family attractions, animal encounters, and resort facilities.
How to Reach There: Fly into Leeds Bradford Airport and continue by road.

9. Adventure Island, Southend-on-Sea, England
Known for thrill rides, family attractions, arcades, and seaside entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into London Southend Airport and continue by local transport.

8. Spain

Spain is one of Europe's leading amusement park destinations in 2026, offering world-class theme parks, Mediterranean resorts, water attractions, family entertainment, and immersive themed experiences across Barcelona, Madrid, Tarragona, and Benidorm.

Best Time to Visit
Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lighter crowds, while summer delivers extended hours, festivals, and water park experiences. Spain combines thrilling attractions, Mediterranean weather, family-friendly resorts, and some of Europe's most visited theme parks. Spain offers around 150 amusement and theme parks, combining family entertainment with resort tourism.

Top 10 Amusement Parks

1. PortAventura World, Tarragona, Catalonia
Home to Shambhala, Dragon Khan, Ferrari Land, themed worlds, and family attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Barcelona-El Prat Airport and continue by train or road.

2. Ferrari Land, Tarragona, Catalonia
Features Red Force, Ferrari-themed experiences, simulators, and high-speed attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Barcelona-El Prat Airport and continue to PortAventura World.

3. Parque Warner Madrid, Madrid
Known for DC superheroes, Batman Gotham City Escape, movie-themed attractions, and family rides.
How to Reach There: Fly into Madrid-Barajas Airport and continue by train or taxi.

4. Siam Park, Tenerife, Canary Islands
Offers world-famous water attractions, wave pools, slides, and tropical settings.
How to Reach There: Fly into Tenerife South Airport and continue by road.

5. Tibidabo Amusement Park, Barcelona, Catalonia
Features classic rides, panoramic city views, family attractions, and historic charm.
How to Reach There: Fly into Barcelona-El Prat Airport and continue by public transport.

6. Isla Mágica, Seville, Andalusia
Combines themed attractions, family rides, water experiences, and historical themes.
How to Reach There: Fly into Seville Airport and continue by road.

7. Parque de Atracciones de Madrid, Madrid
Known for thrill rides, roller coasters, Nickelodeon Land, and family entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Madrid-Barajas Airport and continue by metro or taxi.

8. Terra Mítica, Benidorm, Valencia
Features ancient civilization-themed rides, roller coasters, and family attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Alicante Airport and continue by road.

9. Katmandu Park, Mallorca, Balearic Islands
Offers interactive attractions, 4D experiences, family entertainment, and themed adventures.
How to Reach There: Fly into Palma de Mallorca Airport and continue by road.

10. Sendaviva, Navarra
Combines amusement rides, wildlife experiences, family attractions, and outdoor adventures.
How to Reach There: Fly into Pamplona Airport and continue by car.

9. China

China is one of Asia's fastest-growing amusement park destinations in 2026, offering world-class theme parks, immersive entertainment, cutting-edge attractions, cultural experiences, and family resorts across Shanghai, Beijing, Guangzhou, Zhuhai, and Shenzhen. China combines large-scale theme parks, advanced technology, cultural storytelling, and rapidly expanding entertainment destinations. China dominates Asia in scale, with more than 500 amusement and theme parks across the country.

Best Time to Visit
Spring and autumn provide comfortable weather and smaller crowds, while summer brings major events, festivals, and extended park hours.

Top 10 Amusement Parks

1. Shanghai Disneyland, Shanghai
Home to TRON Lightcycle Power Run, Pirates of the Caribbean, Zootopia, and Enchanted Storybook Castle.
How to Reach There: Fly into Shanghai Pudong International Airport and continue by metro or taxi.

2. Universal Beijing Resort, Beijing
Features The Wizarding World of Harry Potter, Transformers Metrobase, Jurassic World, and Minions attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Beijing Capital or Beijing Daxing International Airport.

3. Chimelong Ocean Kingdom, Zhuhai, Guangdong
Known for marine-life exhibits, roller coasters, aquarium experiences, and family attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Zhuhai Jinwan Airport or Hong Kong International Airport.

4. Chimelong Paradise, Guangzhou, Guangdong
Offers major roller coasters, thrill rides, family attractions, and entertainment shows.
How to Reach There: Fly into Guangzhou Baiyun International Airport.

5. Hong Kong Disneyland, Hong Kong
Features World of Frozen, Mystic Manor, Iron Man Experience, and classic Disney attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Hong Kong International Airport and continue by rail.

6. OCT East, Shenzhen, Guangdong
Combines themed attractions, adventure experiences, scenic areas, and family entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport.

7. Happy Valley Beijing, Beijing
Known for roller coasters, themed lands, water attractions, and family rides.
How to Reach There: Fly into Beijing Capital International Airport and continue by metro or taxi.

8. Happy Valley Shanghai, Shanghai
Features thrill rides, roller coasters, family attractions, and seasonal entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Shanghai Hongqiao or Pudong International Airport.

9. Fantawild Adventure, Wuhu, Anhui
Offers technology-driven attractions, themed experiences, family rides, and immersive entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Nanjing Lukou International Airport and continue by rail or road.

10. Sunac Land Guangzhou, Guangdong
Known for indoor attractions, roller coasters, themed zones, and year-round entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Guangzhou Baiyun International Airport and continue by metro or taxi.

10. Singapore

Singapore is one of Asia's premier amusement park destinations in 2026, offering world-class theme parks, immersive attractions, family entertainment, indoor experiences, and waterfront leisure destinations across Sentosa and the city-state. It combines world-class attractions, seamless infrastructure, family-friendly experiences, and year-round accessibility. Despite its size, Singapore hosts over 20 major amusement and themed entertainment parks.

Best Time to Visit
Singapore is a year-round destination, though February to April generally offers drier weather and comfortable conditions for outdoor attractions.

Top 10 Amusement Parks

1. Universal Studios Singapore, Sentosa
Home to Battlestar Galactica, Transformers: The Ride, Jurassic World attractions, and themed movie worlds.
How to Reach There: Fly into Singapore Changi Airport and continue by MRT, taxi, or Sentosa Express.

2. Adventure Cove Waterpark, Sentosa
Features high-speed water slides, snorkeling experiences, wave pools, and family attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Singapore Changi Airport and continue to Sentosa Island.

3. Skyline Luge Singapore, Sentosa
Known for gravity-powered luge rides, scenic chairlifts, and family entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Singapore Changi Airport and travel to Sentosa.

4. KidZania Singapore, Sentosa
Offers interactive role-playing activities, educational attractions, and family experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Singapore Changi Airport and continue to Sentosa. (When operating or reopened.)

5. HydroDash, Sentosa
Features floating obstacle courses, water-based adventures, and family-friendly fun.
How to Reach There: Fly into Singapore Changi Airport and continue to Palawan Beach, Sentosa.

6. Nestopia, Sentosa
Offers outdoor play structures, obstacle courses, and family attractions overlooking the beach.
How to Reach There: Fly into Singapore Changi Airport and continue to Sentosa.

7. Pororo Park Singapore, Marina Square
Known for themed indoor play zones, rides, and children's entertainment.
How to Reach There: Fly into Singapore Changi Airport and continue by MRT or taxi.

8. SuperPark Singapore, Suntec City
Features indoor adventure zones, obstacle courses, sports activities, and family attractions.
How to Reach There: Fly into Singapore Changi Airport and continue by MRT or taxi.

9. Snow City Singapore, Jurong East
Offers snow play experiences, ice attractions, and family-friendly indoor activities.
How to Reach There: Fly into Singapore Changi Airport and continue by MRT or taxi.

10. HeadRock VR, Sentosa
Known for virtual reality adventures, interactive attractions, and immersive entertainment experiences.
How to Reach There: Fly into Singapore Changi Airport and continue to Sentosa Island.

11. Brazil

Brazil is Latin America's leading amusement park destination in 2026, offering world-class roller coasters, water parks, family attractions, wildlife experiences, and entertainment resorts across São Paulo, Santa Catarina, Rio de Janeiro, and Paraná.

Best Time to Visit
April to October offers pleasant weather across most regions, while summer brings water park experiences and holiday entertainment. Brazil combines thrilling attractions, family entertainment, tropical settings, and some of South America's largest theme parks. Brazil's amusement park sector includes an estimated 180 parks and attractions nationwide.

"Today's travelers are looking beyond traditional sightseeing and embracing destinations that deliver excitement, imagination, and memorable shared experiences. The world's leading amusement park destinations are creating powerful tourism ecosystems that combine entertainment, hospitality, technology, and adventure, making them some of the most influential drivers of global travel growth in 2026," said Anup Kumar Keshan, Founder and Editor-in-Chief of Travel and Tour World.

Earlier, Travel And Tour World has published TTW Releases Top 15 Coolcation Destinations in the Americas and Caribbean for 2026, TTW Unveils Top 50 Coolcation Travel Destinations in the World for 2026, TTW Picks Top 30 Wellness Tourism Destinations in Americas and Caribbean for 2026, TTW Ranks the Top 50 Wellness Destinations in the World for 2026 TTW Announces Top 30 Eco-Tourism Destinations in the Americas and Caribbean for 2026, TTW Reveals 50 Best Eco Tourism Destinations in the World for 2026, TTW Unveils Top 50 Airlines in the World for 2026 TTW Reveals Top 100 Convention Centers in the World for 2026.

Research Methodology

The Top 50 Amusement Park Travel Destinations in the World for 2026 were selected through a comprehensive evaluation framework combining tourism performance, amusement park infrastructure, visitor demand, attraction diversity, innovation, and destination appeal. Developed by TTW's editorial and research team, the ranking highlights destinations delivering exceptional entertainment experiences while driving tourism growth and visitor engagement worldwide.

1. Research Design

The study used descriptive, comparative, and evaluative research methods to assess amusement park ecosystems, attraction quality, visitor experiences, tourism infrastructure, and entertainment innovation. Particular emphasis was placed on destinations offering a strong mix of thrill rides, themed attractions, water parks, family entertainment, and immersive experiences.

2. Data Collection

Research incorporated tourism board statistics, theme park operator data, industry reports, travel publications, hospitality studies, visitor reviews, and attendance records. More than 100 destinations were analyzed, with the final Top 50 selected based on attraction quality, tourism significance, market influence, and TTW audience engagement.

3. Data Parameters

Evaluation focused on:

  • Theme Park Infrastructure and Attraction Diversity
  • Visitor Experience and Guest Satisfaction
  • Family and Multi-Generational Appeal
  • Tourism Accessibility and Connectivity
  • Innovation, Technology, and Investment
  • Global Popularity and Visitor Demand
  • Economic and Tourism Impact

4. Analytical Approach

Destinations were benchmarked against regional competitors using standardized indicators covering attraction quality, accessibility, innovation, and tourism performance.

  • North America: USA, Canada, Mexico
  • Europe: France, Germany, United Kingdom, Spain, Italy, Netherlands and others
  • Asia-Pacific: Japan, China, Singapore, South Korea, Australia, Thailand, Malaysia, India
  • Latin America, Middle East & Africa: Brazil, UAE, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Argentina, Colombia, Egypt, Morocco and others

5. Case Studies

Detailed assessments highlighted leading performers including the United States for its unmatched theme park concentration, France for destination-scale entertainment resorts, Japan for immersive storytelling, China for rapid expansion, Singapore for integrated entertainment tourism, and the UAE for next-generation themed attractions.

6. Validation & Reliability

Findings were cross-verified through tourism statistics, industry publications, attendance data, destination performance reports, and traveler feedback. A standardized scoring framework ensured transparency, consistency, regional balance, and credibility, resulting in a reliable and globally representative ranking for 2026.

data.title: Kos Biotechnology Partners Announces Third Closing of Global Life Sciences Fund at $123 Million
data.description:
Kos Biotechnology Partners Announces Third Closing of Global Life Sciences Fund at $123 Million

Kos Biotechnology Partners Announces Third Closing of Global Life Sciences Fund at $123 Million

PR Newswire

NEW YORK and ATHENS, Greece, June 13, 2026

NEW YORK and ATHENS, Greece, June 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Kos Biotechnology Partners ("Kos"), a global life sciences investment firm bridging the US biopharma ecosystem with Europe, today announced the third closing of its inaugural global life sciences fund, bringing total capital commitments to $123M (€106M). Following its initial launch in December 2025, the fund has secured backing from a syndicate of prominent institutional investors—including lead investor Hellenic Development Bank of Investments (HDBI)—and global family offices.

Kos Biotechnology Partners is the first life sciences-dedicated investment fund in Greece. Notably, it represents the largest launch of a venture capital fund in Greece's history and stands as one of the largest first-time life sciences VC launches in Europe.

The firm deploys strategic capital flexibly across all stages of development, from company formation through Series C. Kos targets high-impact investments in three core life sciences subsectors: biotech/novel therapeutics, pharma services, and tech-enabled technologies.

A Convergence of Biology, Data Science, and Investor Pedigree

"The convergence of novel biology with data science and advanced computing is creating unprecedented opportunities to cure complex diseases," said Dr. Simos Simeonidis, Co-Founder and Managing Partner. "Our mission is to back exceptional entrepreneurs redefining the standard of care by leveraging our proprietary international network to connect US drug development know-how with specialized scientific talent."

Kos was founded by Dr. Simos Simeonidis and Alex Tzoukas, combining elite Wall Street institutional investing expertise with deep scientific domain knowledge, with the vision to build the life sciences ecosystem in Greece.

  • Dr. Simos Simeonidis brings biomedical training from Columbia and Harvard Medical Schools, alongside over a decade of Wall Street experience analyzing biotechnology companies at Morgan Stanley, Cowen & Co, and RBC, and a strong private/public investment track record at Sarissa Capital and Ally Bridge Group.
  • Alex Tzoukas brings more than a decade of investment banking and private equity experience in the healthcare sector, drawing from his tenure at Deutsche Bank, MTS Health Partners, and Gurnet Point Capital.
  • Nikos Kostaras joins as the third partner, adding deep operational and strategic expertise, most recently serving as Greece's country head at IQVIA.

"Securing our third closing at $123 million in just a few months validates our cross-border thesis and underscores the immense potential of bridging US scientific innovation and drug development expertise with Europe's advancing life sciences ecosystem," added Alex Tzoukas, Co-Founder and Managing Partner. "Our ability to invest flexibly across all growth stages ensures Kos can function as a committed, long-term operational partner to innovators transforming human health."

Investment Strategy & Operational Momentum

  • Biotech/Novel Therapeutics: Backing first-in-class or best-in-class modalities addressing critical unmet medical needs.
  • Pharma Services: Supporting next-generation contract research, development, and manufacturing platforms to accelerate drug discovery.
  • Tech-Enabled Technologies: Capitalizing on computational tools, data infrastructure, and machine learning models that de-risk clinical development.

Helping to implement and accelerate its global strategy, the fund leverages the expertise of its advisors, comprising a group of prominent industry leaders drawn from the Greek diaspora in the United States. This elite advisory network brings together world-class scientific minds, clinical experts, and corporate biopharma executives who bridge international networks to help portfolio companies scale globally.

Demonstrating strong operational momentum, Kos has already completed its first two investments. This includes leading the recent financing round for tech-enabled pharma services provider Epikast, alongside a strategic investment in a San Francisco-based biotechnology company to be announced soon. Multiple additional investments are slated for completion later this year.

About Kos Biotechnology Partners

Kos Biotechnology Partners is a global life sciences investment firm based in New York and Athens, Greece. Founded by Dr. Simos Simeonidis and Alex Tzoukas, Kos manages $123 million in assets and invests across all stages of development in therapeutic breakthroughs, innovative pharma services, and tech-driven platforms that advance human health.

data.title: Lilly to present initial clinical data for first-in-class type II JAK2 inhibitor in patients with previously treated myelofibrosis at the 2026 EHA Annual Meeting
data.description:
Lilly to present initial clinical data for first-in-class type II JAK2 inhibitor in patients with previously treated myelofibrosis at the 2026 EHA Annual Meeting

Lilly to present initial clinical data for first-in-class type II JAK2 inhibitor in patients with previously treated myelofibrosis at the 2026 EHA Annual Meeting

PR Newswire

INDIANAPOLIS, June 13, 2026

INDIANAPOLIS, June 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Eli Lilly and Company (NYSE: LLY) today announced new data from the Phase 1 AJX-101 study showing that its investigational type II JAK2 inhibitor (AJ1-11095) demonstrated an encouraging safety profile and promising clinical activity in patients with myelofibrosis who have been failed by a type I JAK2 inhibitor. This first-in-class type II JAK2 inhibitor was designed to selectively bind the type II conformation of the JAK2 kinase in order to potentially provide greater efficacy than existing therapies and a novel treatment option for patients who become resistant to type I JAK2 inhibitors. Lilly recently added this program to its pipeline following the completion of the acquisition of Ajax Therapeutics, Inc.

These data will be highlighted in an oral presentation at the 2026 European Hematology Association (EHA) Annual Meeting taking place in Stockholm, Sweden (Abstract number: S218) and featured in the meeting's press program.

"Patients with myelofibrosis who have been previously treated with an existing type I JAK2 inhibitor face very limited treatment options, highlighting an urgent need for new therapies," said John Mascarenhas, MD, professor of medicine, Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai and principal investigator of the AJX-101 study. "These early clinical findings suggest that selective targeting of the type II conformation of JAK2 may provide a differentiated approach. With an encouraging safety profile, meaningful spleen size reduction, symptom improvement, and decrease in underlying mutant disease burden, these data, while early, point to the potential to meaningfully impact treatment options for people with certain myeloproliferative neoplasms."

AJX-101 is the first clinical trial to evaluate a type II selective JAK2 inhibitor in patients with myelofibrosis. The trial enrolled 23 patients across five dose levels (25, 50, 75, 100, and 125 mg once daily) in its dose escalation phase. Patients had received a median of two prior therapies, and all had previously received a type I JAK2 inhibitor. The trial enrolled patients across all major myelofibrosis subtypes and driver mutations.

AJ1-11095 demonstrated responses across the standard efficacy endpoints of spleen volume reduction and symptom improvement.1 The SVR35 rate, a reduction in spleen volume of at least 35%, was observed as best response in 70% of patients. The TSS50 rate, indicating at least a 50% improvement in symptom burden, was also seen in 70% of patients at week 12. In addition, reductions in driver mutation variant allele frequency (VAF) were observed in 21 out of 23 patients. Among the 17 patients who reached week 24 of treatment, 59% saw a reduction of 20% or greater and 35% saw a reduction of 50% or greater, including JAK2, MPL, and CALR type 1 and type 2 mutations. VAF reductions are uncommonly observed with existing type I JAK2 inhibitors.2

The overall safety profile for the medicine was generally manageable. No dose-limiting toxicities were observed, and most patients enrolled in the dose escalation phase remain on study (78%).3 The most common treatment-emergent adverse events across all dose levels included anemia, dysgeusia, decreased platelet count, and increased alanine aminotransferase.

"The depth of response seen across spleen, symptoms, and VAF from these early phase results is in excess of what has been seen historically in this disease setting," said Jacob Van Naarden, executive vice president and president of Lilly Oncology. "These data provide clear proof of concept for what this selective type II JAK2 inhibitor could mean for patients with myelofibrosis and shed light on the conviction we brought to the acquisition of Ajax. With this program now officially part of Lilly's pipeline, we are committed to rapidly advancing it through clinical development and further exploring its potential to meaningfully improve outcomes for people with myeloproliferative neoplasms across a range of disease settings."

AJ1-11095 is currently being evaluated in an expansion cohort in second-line myelofibrosis, with plans to investigate in patients with high-risk polycythemia vera and those with myelofibrosis who have not yet received a JAK2 inhibitor. Details on the AJX-101 trial can be found by visiting clinicaltrials.gov.

About AJ1-11095
AJ1-11095 is an investigational, oral, first-in-class type II JAK2 inhibitor. AJ1-11095 is designed to bind JAK2 in its inactive conformation — an approach intended to more completely suppress the aberrant signaling that drives myelofibrosis, in contrast to currently approved JAK2 inhibitors that bind JAK2 in its active state. AJ1-11095 demonstrated superior activity compared to ruxolitinib in preclinical models of myelofibrosis. AJ1-11095 is currently being studied in AJX-101, a global, open-label, multicenter, Phase 1 study in patients with primary myelofibrosis, post-polycythemia vera myelofibrosis, or post-essential thrombocythemia myelofibrosis who have previously been treated with a type I JAK2 inhibitor, NCT06343805.

About Lilly
Lilly is a medicine company turning science into healing to make life better for people around the world. We've been pioneering life-changing discoveries for 150 years, and today our medicines help tens of millions of people across the globe. Harnessing the power of biotechnology, chemistry and genetic medicine, our scientists are urgently advancing new discoveries to solve some of the world's most significant health challenges: redefining diabetes care; treating obesity and curtailing its most devastating long-term effects; advancing the fight against Alzheimer's disease; providing solutions to some of the most debilitating immune system disorders; and transforming the most difficult-to-treat cancers into manageable diseases. With each step toward a healthier world, we're motivated by one thing: making life better for millions more people. That includes delivering innovative clinical trials that reflect the diversity of our world and working to ensure our medicines are accessible and affordable. To learn more, visit Lilly.com and Lilly.com/news, or follow us on Facebook, Instagram, and LinkedIn. P-LLY

Solely for convenience, the trademarks and trade names in this press release are referred to without the ® and ™ symbols, but such references should not be construed as any indicator that the company or, to the extent applicable, their respective owners will not assert, to the fullest extent under applicable law, the company's or their rights thereto. data.title: Incyte Announces New Positive Data at EHA 2026 Showed INCA033989 Achieved Rapid, Robust and Sustained Clinical and Molecular Responses and Was Well Tolerated in Patients with Myelofibrosis and Essential Thrombocythemia
data.description:
Incyte Announces New Positive Data at EHA 2026 Showed INCA033989 Achieved Rapid, Robust and Sustained Clinical and Molecular Responses and Was Well Tolerated in Patients with Myelofibrosis and Essential Thrombocythemia

  • In myelofibrosis (MF), INCA033989 delivered rapid and durable clinical benefits including meaningful spleen volume reductions, symptom improvement and anemia responses, both as a monotherapy and in combination with ruxolitinib
  • In essential thrombocythemia (ET), 87% of patients achieved a hematologic response, including 70% complete responses; responses were rapid (median ~2 weeks to a durable complete hematologic response) and durable (median response duration of 23 weeks)
  • Across MF and ET, INCA033989 consistently reduced mutant CALR (mutCALR) variant allele frequency (VAF) in most evaluable patients, with reductions correlating with clinical responses and supporting its potential for disease modification
  • First-in-class mutCALR-targeted antibody shows potential to modify disease biology in both MF and ET
  • INCA033989 demonstrated a favorable and manageable safety profile with no dose-limiting toxicities, with most patients with MF and ET continuing treatment

Incyte (Nasdaq:INCY) today announced updated clinical data from two Phase 1 studies evaluating the safety, tolerability and efficacy of INCA033989, a first-in-class mutant calreticulin (mutCALR)-targeted monoclonal antibody, in patients with mutCALR-expressing myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPNs). INCA033989 demonstrated rapid, clinically meaningful responses and consistent molecular activity across both myelofibrosis (MF) and essential thrombocythemia (ET), with convergent evidence supporting the potential for disease modification.

These findings are being presented in oral and poster presentations at the European Hematology Association (EHA) 2026 Congress in Stockholm, Sweden (Session: Myeloproliferative neoplasms – Clinical, Presentation numbers: S216, PS1983, PF884).

“The data presented at EHA 2026 demonstrate clinically meaningful and consistent responses with INCA033989 across both myelofibrosis and essential thrombocythemia,” said Pablo J. Cagnoni, M.D., President of Incyte and Global Head of Research and Development. “What distinguishes INCA033989 is its potential to deliver disease control while targeting the biology that drives it. We remain on track to initiate our pivotal ET study by mid-2026 and are actively engaging regulators on a pivotal MF program.”

Results in Patients with Myelofibrosis (MF)

The safety, tolerability, and efficacy of INCA033989 in Type 1 and non-Type 1 patients with MF harboring a CALR mutation is being evaluated in two ongoing Phase 1 studies. Results demonstrate that INCA033989 delivers broad, clinically meaningful improvements across spleen volume, symptom burden and anemia in patients with MF. As a monotherapy and in combination with ruxolitinib, INCA033989 had a manageable safety profile and the majority of patients remained on treatment – no dose-limiting toxicities were observed, and a maximum tolerated dose was not reached.

Monotherapy: INCA033989 was evaluated as monotherapy in patients who were resistant, refractory or intolerant to JAK inhibitor treatment after >12 weeks (JAK R/R/I), or ineligible to JAK inhibitor therapy. The dose escalation cohort evaluated INCA033989 from 24-3500 mg, and the dose expansion cohort evaluated 250 mg and 2000 mg.

INCA033989 monotherapy demonstrated durable clinical benefit, with clinically meaningful improvements across spleen volume, symptoms and anemia across both JAK R/R/I and JAK ineligible patients.

  • Spleen Volume Reduction (SVR): Rapid and robust spleen volume reductions were observed in patients, with 55% (38/69) and 39% (27/69) of patients achieving the best SVR25 and SVR35 reduction, respectively. At Week 24, 27% (17/62) patients achieved SVR35, including 47% (8/17) JAK ineligible and 20% (9/45) JAK R/R/I. Robust responses were observed in JAK ineligible patients regardless of mutation type (60% [6/10] Type-1 vs. 29% [2/7] non-Type 1). In JAK R/R/I patients, clinically meaningful reductions were observed in 31% (8/26) of Type-1 patients across all evaluated doses at Week 24, and 33% (1/3) of non-Type-1 patients evaluated at 2500 mg, the highest evaluated dose.
  • Symptom Improvement: Improvements in symptoms were also observed in the majority of patients, with 53% of patients achieving at least a 50% best TSS reduction (TSS50). At Week 24, 32% of patients achieved TSS50, including 29% and 33% of JAK ineligible and JAK R/R/I patients, respectively.
  • Anemia: Rapid and durable anemia improvements were observed in most patients, with anemia response occurring in 60% of evaluable anemic patients, and 52% of patients achieved a major anemia response. Improvements in anemia were observed across patients regardless of prior JAK exposure, including 63% of JAK R/R/I patients and 55% of JAK ineligible patients.
  • Molecular: Consistent reductions in variant allele frequency (VAF) were observed across most patients, regardless of prior JAK exposure and mutation type, with 89% of patients achieving a reduction in whole blood mutCALR VAF (Type 1: 90%, Non-Type 1: 88%), and 81% of patients achieving a ≥25% reduction in mutCALR peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMC) from baseline (Type 1: 62%, Non-Type 1: 38%).

INCA033989 was generally well-tolerated, with 84% (70/83) of patients remaining on therapy at the time of the data cut off. Treatment emergent adverse events (TEAEs) occurred in 92% (76) of patients, with 27% (22) of patients experiencing Grade ≥3 TEAEs, the most frequent of which were cytopenias. No dose-limiting toxicities were observed, and discontinuations due to TEAEs were limited (n=2).

Combination therapy: INCA033989 (dose range: 70 to 2,500 mg) was evaluated in combination with ruxolitinib in patients with MF who experience a suboptimal response to ruxolitinib monotherapy. INCA033989 demonstrated additive, multi-domain clinical activity in patients when administered in combination with ruxolitinib.

  • SVR: At Week 24, 55% (11/20) and 30% (6/20) of patients achieved SVR25 and SVR35, respectively.
  • Symptom Improvement: 31% (5/16) of patients achieved TSS50 at Week 24.
  • Anemia: Anemia response occurred in 35% (6/17) of evaluable anemic patients.

INCA033989 in combination with ruxolitinib was generally well-tolerated, with 76% (16) of patients remaining on treatment at the time of the data cut off.In the combination arm (n=21), all patients experienced TEAEs. Grade ≥3 TEAEs were reported in 67% (14) of patients, most commonly anemia (33%).

Translational data

  • Clinical response occurred regardless of mutational complexity with SVR, anemia and molecular responses observed in patients with and without high molecular risk (HMR) mutations.
  • 93% of patients with HMR had a reduction in whole blood mutCALR VAF, as did 88% of those without HMR mutations.
  • A reduction in mutCALR-positive hematopoietic stem and progenitor cells (HSPCs) was also seen, indicating activity at the level of disease-initiating cells.

"Patients with CALR-mutated MF have distinct disease biology and often respond poorly to available therapies, underscoring the need for treatments targeting the underlying driver of disease," said Claire Harrison, M.D., Professor of MPNs and Deputy Chief Medical Officer, Guy’s and St. Thomas’ NHS Foundation Trust. "What stands out in these data is that INCA033989 produced rapid and robust spleen, symptom and anemia responses, alongside reductions in mutCALR allele burden regardless of HMR mutations, pointing to activity at the level of the disease-initiating clone."

Results in Patients with Essential Thrombocythemia

Inpatients with ET, INCA033989 demonstrated rapid, deep and durable hematologic and molecular responses across both Type 1 and non-Type 1 CALR patients, supporting potential for disease modification in a population resistant or intolerant to prior cytoreductive therapy.

Hematologic Response:

  • Across doses, 70% (80/114) of patients achieved a complete hematologic response (CHR, platelet count ≤400 × 109/L and leukocytes <10 × 109/L) and 87% achieved complete or partial hematologic response (CHR/PHR, platelet count ≤600 × 109/L and leukocytes <10 × 109/L).
  • 81% of patients with Type 1 mutCALR achieved a durable (>12 weeks) CHR at 750 mg and above; and 50% of patients with non–Type 1 mutCALR achieved a durable CHR/PHR at 2500 mg. The median time to onset of durable CHR was 2.1 weeks.

Molecular Response and Disease Biology:

  • ≥25% reduction in mutCALR VAF correlated with durable CHR (nominal P<0.0001, n=103).
  • Of the patients who achieved a CHR and had ≥1 post-baseline VAF assessment, 73% achieved ≥25% reduction in VAF.
  • Durable molecular response was observed in both Type 1 and non–Type 1 mutCALR.
  • A reduction in mutCALR megakaryocytes was seen in both Type 1 and non-Type 1 patients treated with INCA033989

INCA033989 was well tolerated with 95% of patients remaining on treatment. The median duration of INCA033989 exposure was 8.1 months (range from 0.59 to 27.0 months). A low incidence of Grade ≥3 adverse events was observed (19%); the most common were neutropenia (4.4%) and lipase increase (3.5%). Grade ≥3 cytopenia TEAEs occurred in 6% (7/114) of patients; no Grade ≥3 thrombocytopenia TEAEs were observed.

“In patients with ET who were resistant to or intolerant of prior cytoreductive therapy, INCA033989 resulted in rapid and durable normalization of platelet counts with accompanying molecular responses, with the majority of patients achieving a CHR," said John Mascarenhas, M.D., Professor of Medicine at the Icahn School of Medicine at Mt. Sinai and Director, Center of Excellence for Blood Cancers and Myeloid Disorders, The Tisch Cancer Institute. "As there are currently no mutation-specific treatments available for patients with ET, this approach is critically important for this high-risk patient population. These results provide a strong foundation for advancing INCA033989 into a registrational Phase 3 study."

In November of 2025, INCA033989 was granted Breakthrough Therapy designation by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for the treatment of patients with ET harboring a Type 1 CALR mutation who are resistant or intolerant to at least one cytoreductive therapy. A Phase 3 study of INCA033989 in mutCALR positive patients with ET who are resistant or intolerant to at least one prior cytoreductive therapy (EXCALIBUR-ET2, NCT07623200) is being initiated in mid-2026.

More information regarding the EHA 2026 Congress can be found on the EHA website: https://ehaweb.org/connect-network/eha2026-congress.

About Myeloproliferative Neoplasms (MPNs) and Mutations in Calreticulin (mutCALR)

Calreticulin (CALR) is a protein involved in the regulation of cellular calcium levels and normal protein folding. Somatic, or non-inherited, DNA mutations in the CALR gene (mutCALR) can result in abnormal protein function and lead to the development of myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPNs),1 a closely related group of clonal blood cancers in which the bone marrow functions abnormally, overproducing blood cells.2,3 Among two types of MPNs, essential thrombocythemia (ET) and myelofibrosis (MF), mutCALR drives 25-35% of all cases.4 In MF, it is estimated that 70-83% of CALR mutations in the U.S. are identified as Type 1, with 15-30% identified as non-Type 1.4,5 There are currently no targeted therapies for CALR mutations.

Incyte is at the forefront of developing novel therapies for patients with mutCALR ET or MF that target only malignant cells, sparing normal cells, including INCA033989, a first-in-class, mutCALR-specific therapy. INCA033989 received Breakthrough Therapy designation by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for the treatment of patients with ET harboring a Type 1 CALR mutation who are resistant or intolerant to at least one cytoreductive therapy. A Phase 3 study of INCA033989 in patients with ET with a Type 1 or non-Type 1 CALR mutation who are resistant or intolerant to at least one cytoreductive therapy is being initiated (EXCALIBUR-ET2, NCT07623200).

About the INCA33989-101 & INCA33989-102 Trials

The clinical trial program for INCA033989 includes two multicenter, open-label Phase 1 studies, INCA33989-101 (NCT05936359) and INCA33989-102 (NCT06034002). The studies are evaluating the safety, tolerability and efficacy of INCA033989 in ~455 adult (≥18 years old) patients with mutCALR-expressing myeloproliferative neoplasms (MPNs), including essential thrombocythemia (ET) and myelofibrosis (MF).

The primary endpoint of the studies is measured by the number of participants with dose limiting toxicities (DLTs), treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs) and the number of participants with TEAEs leading to dose modification or discontinuation. Secondary endpoints include response rates, mean change of ET total symptom score, percentage of MF patients achieving spleen volume reduction, MF patient anemia response, mean change in disease-related allele burden and various pharmacokinetics measures.

For more information on the studies, please visit: https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT05936359 and https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT06034002.

About Incyte®

Incyte is redefining what’s possible in biopharmaceutical innovation. Through deep scientific expertise and a relentless focus on patients, we have built an established portfolio of first-in-class medicines and an extensive portfolio of next-generation medicines across our key franchises: Hematology, Oncology and Inflammation & Autoimmunity.

To learn more, visit Incyte.com and Investor.Incyte.com. Follow us on social media: LinkedIn, X and Instagram.

data.title: Saudi Arabia to Host Second Consultative Meeting for the 11th World Water Forum
data.description:
World Water Forum

JEDDAH, SAUDI ARABIA / ACCESS Newswire / June 13, 2026 / Saudi Arabia is intensifying its early preparations to host the 11th World Water Forum in 2027, including the Second Consultative Meeting held as part of Saudi Water Week. The meeting represents a key milestone in preparations for this global event. It will bring together experts and decision-makers from around the world, as well as representatives of government entities, the private sector, and international organizations.

The meeting aims to align visions, coordinate efforts among stakeholders, and discuss the Forum's main priorities and themes. It will also address organizational and technical frameworks, as well as partnership and cooperation mechanisms, to help ensure the delivery of a distinguished edition that reflects Saudi Arabia's position and role in water-related issues.

This early action and intensified preparation for hosting this major global event reflect the Kingdom's commitment to a collaborative approach, to strengthening international dialogue on water challenges, and to promoting practical and sustainable solutions that support global water security.

The 11th World Water Forum will be held in Riyadh in March 2027 under the theme "Action for a Better Tomorrow", in partnership with the World Water Council. The Forum will bring together broad participation from the public and private sectors, international and local organizations, and experts, officials, and stakeholders interested in water issues at the local and international levels.

The Forum is the largest global event dedicated to water management, providing a platform for the exchange of ideas and knowledge, and for cooperation among stakeholders worldwide. It also offers opportunities to showcase international best practices and explore avenues for collaboration to ensure the sustainable management of water resources around the world.

data.title: My elderly mother and I own a home together. Will Medicaid force its sale?
data.description:
By Quentin Fottrell

'My sister thinks Medicaid will take the house my mom and I own together'

"None of us is wealthy. We don't have trusts or LLCs." (Photo subject is a model.)

Dear Quentin,

Fourteen years ago, my mom and I bought a small house together (I live in another state). Both of our names are on the title "with rights of survivorship." She has reached the age where a nursing home is becoming increasingly likely.

With my dad and my aunt and uncle, Medicaid took everything, including their homes, to pay for nursing-home care. My sister thinks Medicaid will take the house my mom and I own together, claiming half of its value every year and effectively reducing my portion of ownership to nothing.

My other sister spoke with someone at one of the nursing homes, who said I may be able to buy out my mom's half based on a fair-market-value appraisal, if I act soon. None of us is wealthy. We don't have trusts or LLCs. What are our options?

We live in a small town in Nebraska.

The Daughter

Don't miss: 'I'm experiencing issues with arthritis': I'm 68 with $3 million saved. Why am I not ready for a life of leisure?

You can email The Moneyist with any financial and ethical questions at qfottrell@marketwatch.com. The Moneyist regrets he cannot reply to questions individually.

Some people in your situation establish an irrevocable trust early enough to avoid the five-year look-back rule.

Dear Daughter,

You are correct to act sooner rather than later, especially if your mom is declining.

While a person's home may not be counted toward Medicaid's asset limit, which varies by state, it is not exempt from the Medicaid Estate Recovery Program. After your mother's death, if she received Medicaid-funded nursing-home care, the Nebraska Medicaid agency may attempt to be reimbursed for care costs out of whatever remains of the estate, including her interest in the home.

First, a warning: A bona fide sale for fair market value is generally treated differently from a gift for Medicaid purposes. But that raises another problem. The proceeds from such a sale could become "countable assets" and may affect your mother's ongoing Medicaid eligibility.

Some people establish an irrevocable trust early enough to avoid the five-year look-back rule. Assets properly transferred into a Medicaid Asset Protection Trust (MAPT) generally are no longer owned by the individual and, as such, may not be counted for Medicaid eligibility purposes. Transfers made within Medicaid's five-year look-back period can trigger a penalty period delaying eligibility for benefits.

Nebraska uses "expanded estate recovery" rather than "probate estate recovery," meaning that even if your mother's share of the house transfers to you automatically at her death, Medicaid may still be able to seek recovery against certain assets in which she had a legal interest at the time of death. Whether a jointly owned property with rights of survivorship is subject to recovery can depend on the language of the deed and other variables. Talk to an estate-planning attorney, preferably one who specializes in Medicaid.

New powers to reclaim Medicaid costs

Almost a decade ago, Nebraska made it easier to recover Medicaid costs. Previously, the state could claim only assets that passed through probate. Now, a recipient's "estate" includes property in which the person had a legal interest at the time of death. Joint tenancies, living trusts, life estates and transfer-on-death deeds are no longer necessarily protected from recovery, depending on the circumstances.

Although exemptions still exist for hardship waivers, federally protected pensions and certain burial plans, the state now has the power to reach assets that were previously off-limits in some situations, according to Jacobsen Orr, a law firm based in Kearney, Neb. However, transfers can have unintended consequences, including Medicaid penalties and tax implications, so make sure you seek out legal counsel before you make any sudden moves.

"Certain home transfers will also not be subject to estate recovery if the home is transferred to a spouse, a child under 21 years of age, a child who is blind or disabled or a child who resided in the home for at least two years before institutionalization and provided care that allowed the parent to remain at home rather than enter a nursing facility, so long as the statutory requirements are met," the law firm says.

Nebraska, Florida, New York and California, among other states, exempt a primary residence from assets counted for Medicaid eligibility under certain circumstances. In many states, if a person or their spouse needs to live in the home, or if the person expresses an intent to return home, the residence can remain exempt while determining eligibility. "Once the intent to return is nullified, a home becomes a countable asset that can be used to pay for long-term care," AgingCare says.

The fact that a home may be exempt from eligibility while a person is alive and applying for Medicaid does not necessarily mean the property will be protected from recovery efforts after their death. Still, you and your mother purchased this house together 14 years ago. If you contributed to the original purchase and have been a bona fide co-owner since the acquisition, that history could affect the outcome.

Related: My daughter has $500K in med-school expenses. Can my wife and I afford to pay it off?

NEW YORK and CHICAGO, June 13, 2026

PLEASE CLICK HERE TO SUBMIT YOUR CONTACT INFORMATION

NEW YORK and CHICAGO, June 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Wolf Haldenstein Adler Freeman & Herz LLP ("Wolf Haldenstein"), a preeminent national consumer rights law, is investigating claims on behalf of people who have been impacted by Minnesota Epilepsy Group ("MEG") data breach.

MEG, headquartered in Roseville, Minnesota, announced that the personal information of individuals may have been stolen as part of a data breach which occurred in March 2026.

MEG is notifying affected people that their personal information, including at least names, addresses, dates of birth, Social Security numbers, medical treatment information, and health insurance information, may have been stolen. 

If you have received a recent notice of the data breach and have experienced recent concerning activity, it is possible that your personal information was compromised and is being offered for sale on the dark web.

data.title: The Football Stars Missing This Summer's Cup Receive an Invitation from Ecuador
data.description:
The Football Stars Missing This Summer's Cup Receive an Invitation from Ecuador

The Football Stars Missing This Summer's Cup Receive an Invitation from Ecuador

PR Newswire

GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador, June 13, 2026

GUAYAQUIL, Ecuador, June 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Ecuador's Secretary of Tourism has launched Ecuador 2026, an international tourism campaign that uses football as a platform to promote the country to audiences around the world during one of the sport's most visible moments.

The initiative is aimed at a group of internationally recognized football players who will not be participating in this summer's major international tournament. Through personalized billboards and messages displayed in the cities where these players live and play, Ecuador invites them to consider the country as a destination during their time off.

While the messages are addressed to the players themselves, the campaign is also designed to reach the millions of fans who encounter the billboards in those same cities. By leveraging the visibility of global football stars, Ecuador 2026 seeks to position the country as a destination for international travelers, showcasing its unique combination of biodiversity, culture, gastronomy, and natural attractions.

"Ecuador is a country where travelers can experience volcanoes, beaches, the Amazon rainforest, historic cities and the Galápagos Islands within a single journey," said Ecuador's Secretary of Tourism. "Through this campaign, we wanted to use football's global reach to introduce Ecuador to new audiences and invite visitors to discover everything our country has to offer."

As part of the initiative, Ecuador's Secretary of Tourism is open to facilitating visits for any invited stars interested in experiencing the country firsthand. The campaign, however, is primarily focused on promoting Ecuador internationally and inspiring travelers from the players' home countries and host cities to consider Ecuador for future trips.
